name: 50D
notes: Daylight-balanced (5500K) color negative motion picture emulsion (Kodak 50D-derived), remjet removed for safe C-41 processing. EI 50/18deg; usable EI 12-100 without push. World's finest grain color negative film per manufacturer. Acetate base with halation effect.

name: 400D
notes: 400Dynamic. Daylight-balanced color negative motion picture emulsion, remjet removed for C-41. Base ISO 400, rateable 200-800 without push, push up to 3 stops. Process-surviving anti-static lubricant coating. Halation at overexposed highlights.

name: 800T
notes: 800Tungsten. Tungsten-balanced color negative motion picture emulsion, remjet (remjet backing) removed for safe C-41 photo-lab processing. Push up to 3200 ISO. Acetate base; characteristic halation. Also processable in ECN-2/Cs2.

name: BwXX
notes: Rebranded Kodak Double-X 5222 motion picture B&W stock. Panchromatic. Variable base sensitivity: ISO 250 daylight (5500K) / ISO 200 tungsten (3200K); rateable up to EI 1600 with push. Acetate base, DX-coded 135.

name: Color Negative 100
notes: Color Negative 100. Standard C-41. Extra-fine grain, low ISO, vivid colors. Offered in 35 mm and 120.

name: Color Negative 400
notes: Color Negative 400. Standard C-41. Fine grain, versatile. Offered in 35 mm and 120.

name: Color Negative 800
notes: Color Negative 800. Standard C-41. High-speed, good low-light performance. Offered in 35 mm and 120.

name: Lady Grey 400
notes: Lady Grey B&W ISO 400, 35 mm. Rebranded emulsion (re-spooled other manufacturer's B&W emulsion); Lomography does not name the parent stock.

name: Earl Grey 100
notes: Earl Grey B&W ISO 100, 35 mm. Rebranded emulsion (re-spooled other manufacturer's B&W emulsion); Lomography does not name the parent stock.

name: Berlin Kino 400
notes: Berlin Kino B&W ISO 400. Panchromatic cine emulsion; pushable to 800/1600/3200 per manufacturer. Suited to color filters. Offered in 35 mm and 120.

name: Potsdam Kino 100
notes: Potsdam Kino B&W ISO 100. Triacetate-based B&W cine emulsion, medium speed, heavy silver-halide composition. Offered in 35 mm and 120.

name: Fantome Kino 8
notes: Fantome Kino B&W ISO 8, 35 mm. High-contrast B&W cine emulsion. Part of Lomography Kino B&W film family.

name: LomoChrome Metropolis
notes: 2021 LomoChrome Metropolis. Experimental color-shift color negative film, extended EI 100-400, standard C-41. Punchy contrast, desaturated muted tones with pops of color. Offered in 35 mm, 120, 110.

name: LomoChrome Purple
notes: 2021 LomoChrome Purple Petillant. Color-shift color negative film (green-to-purple infrared-like shift), extended EI 100-400, standard C-41. Offered in 35 mm, 120, 110.

name: LomoChrome Turquoise
notes: 2021 LomoChrome Turquoise. Color-shift color negative film, extended EI 100-400, standard C-41 (non-DX-coded, 36 exp). Fine grain. Offered in 35 mm, 120, 110.

name: Redscale XR 50-200
notes: Redscale XR. Redscale color negative film (film wound emulsion-reversed), extended EI 50-200, standard C-41. Glowing red/orange/yellow tones. Offered in 35 mm and 120.
